body:
Fixes #1649.

## Summary

Converts the `hive-screens.sh` script that previously lived inside `README.md`
into a first-class JavaScript bin command shipped with `@link-assistant/hive-mind`.

- `--list`, `--enter`, and `--close` all route through a **single shared
  matching predicate** (`sessionMatches` in `src/hive-screens.lib.mjs`). A
  session visible in `--list` is guaranteed to be the same set that
  `--enter` and `--close` will act on — making `--list` the safe preview
  for the dangerous `--close`, exactly as requested in the issue.
- Selection flags `--oldest` (default), `--newest`, and `--all` are
  preserved 1:1 from the sh script.
- Scrollback capture, non-printable stripping, and extraction of the last
  `Full log file:` path and `Issue:` URL all match the legacy behaviour.

## Files

- `src/hive-screens.lib.mjs` — arg parsing, `screen -ls` listing,
  scrollback capture, shared matching predicate, orchestrator.
- `src/hive-screens.mjs` — thin bin wrapper (same split as
  `configure-claude.mjs`).
- `package.json` — new `"hive-screens"` entry under `bin`, and
  `build:pre` marks it executable on release.
- `README.md` — replaces the embedded sh script with a short usage block
  for `hive-screens`.
- `tests/test-hive-screens.mjs` — unit tests for arg parsing, the shared
  predicate, listing, orchestration, and a bin smoke test.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-1649/` — deep-dive case study: requirements,
  canonical sh-script behaviour, solution plan, survey of existing
  components, and raw GitHub data.
- `.changeset/hive-screens-command.md` — minor release note.

## How to reproduce / verify

### Unit tests

```sh
node tests/test-hive-screens.mjs
# → "All hive-screens tests passed"
```

The full suite still passes:

```sh
npm test    # 41 passed, 0 failed across telegram/solve/queue suites
npm run lint
npm run format:check
```

### Manual smoke test

```sh
# Should print help and exit 0
node src/hive-screens.mjs --help

# Should reject missing action with exit 1
node src/hive-screens.mjs
# → stderr: "Must specify --list, --enter, or --close"

# Safe preview of any finished, mergeable detached screen sessions
node src/hive-screens.mjs --list --all
```

## Test plan

- [x] `parseHiveScreensArgs` covers every flag combination (action,
      selection, conflict, unknown option, help).
- [x] `sessionMatches` asserts the case-insensitive predicate for both
      `PR is mergeable!` and `PR merged!`, and rejects partial matches.
- [x] `listDetachedSessions` handles detached-only filtering, sorting,
      and the "no sessions" exit path.
- [x] `findMatchingSessions` (the real helper) exercised end-to-end
      through fake `exec` + `fs` stubs to assert the hardcopy pipeline.
- [x] `runHiveScreens` verified for `--list` (no side effects), `--close`
      (emits `screen -X stuff $'exit\n'`), and `--enter` (spawns screen).
- [x] Bin smoke test spawning `src/hive-screens.mjs` with no args and
      `--help`.
- [x] `npm run lint` and `npm run format:check` clean.

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1649

…649)

Ports the `hive-screens.sh` script previously embedded in README.md into a
real JS bin command shipped with `@link-assistant/hive-mind`. Supports
`--list` (safe preview), `--enter`, and `--close`, all routed through the
same `sessionMatches` predicate so that anything visible under `--list`
is guaranteed to be actionable by `--enter` and `--close`. Selection
flags `--oldest` (default), `--newest`, and `--all` are preserved.

Also updates README.md to document the new command instead of printing
the sh script, adds a case study under docs/case-studies/issue-1649,
and covers the matching + orchestration surface with unit tests.

title: feat(hive-screens): convert sh script to hive-screens bin command
